Cucumella, also known as Calabash Gourd, is a type of vining plant belonging to the Cucurbitaceae family, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Cucumella include flavonoids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional African medicine, Cucumella is often prepared into a decoction or tincture, and is also used in topical creams and ointments to treat skin conditions and wounds.
